key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration classification across fundamentally different welding processes with strong accuracy gains.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring from one process, such as TIG, to another, such as laser welding.
method: UDA for cross-process penetration status prediction
Gradual source domain expansion to broaden source knowledge
UMAP-based feature alignment check for domain invariance
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process tasks, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, with large improvements over the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on how similar the target welding conditions are to the source data, so robustness across unseen materials, settings, or sensor setups should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
